definitions.technical: A firm registered with the CFTC that accepts orders to buy or sell commodity futures and options. Under the GENIUS Act, stablecoins from non-permitted issuers cannot be used as margin or collateral by these firms.
definitions.lay: A company registered with the CFTC to handle customers' commodity futures trades. The GENIUS Act restricts these firms from accepting stablecoins issued by non-compliant companies as collateral for trades.
definitions.parenthetical: (a CFTC-registered firm handling commodity futures orders, restricted from accepting non-permitted stablecoins as collateral)
definitions.legal_significance: The restriction on futures commission merchants accepting non-permitted stablecoins as margin creates a market-based enforcement mechanism. Issuers that lose permitted status will find their tokens excluded from commodity derivatives markets.
